title: The Prime Day TV Deals Worth Your Money
author: John Brandon
text: Amazon Prime Day June 2026Absolute Best Amazon Prime Day DealsYeti Cooler DealsOura Ring DealsApple DealsOur Favorite Espresso Machine Is on SaleDeals Under $100Apple Watch DealsHeadphones DealsVacuum DealsAir Conditioner DealsSmart Bird Feeder DealsBeauty Tech DealsAll products featured on WIRED are independently selected by our editors. However, we may receive compensation from retailers and/or from purchases of products through these links. Learn more.Ready to upgrade your television? This is an excellent time to buy a new TV or streaming device. Running from June 23 through June 26, the Prime Day TV deals are phenomenal—especially on art televisions like the Samsung Frame Pro and Amazon’s new Ember Artline.Salivating over that new QLED or just need an entry-level TV for the kitchen? While Amazon has several of its own Fire TV models on sale, the latest sets from TCL, Hisense, and even Samsung are also heavily reduced—in some cases by almost 40 percent off. It's a good time to upgrade your big screen because some the best Prime Day tech deals are on TVs.Also be sure to check out our roundup of the Absolute Best Prime Day Deals.Updated 6:40 am ET, June 25: We've added the Fire TV Stick 4K Plus and updated prices and links throughout.Prime Day TV DealsPhotograph: John BrandonPhotograph: John BrandonPhotograph: John BrandonPhotograph: John BrandonSamsungFrame Pro$2,098 $1,498 (29% off) Amazon$1,600 SamsungEven though it’s not the brand-new 2026 model, Samsung’s Frame Pro from 2025 is still a stellar option. Artwork looks stunning on the 65-inch matte display, especially when you are done watching Netflix and looking at Van Gogh oil paintings. Like the Amazon Ember Artline (see below), Samsung includes a wall-mounting bracket that makes this model sit close to the wall.Photograph: John BrandonPhotograph: John BrandonPhotograph: John BrandonAmazonEmber Artline TV$1,100 $900 (18% off) Amazon (65-inch)$900 $700 (22% off) Amazon (55-inch)Most PopularGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeGearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell DealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarIf you choose the 55-inch model of this art television, the Prime deal price is just $700. That’s several hundred dollars cheaper than the Samsung Frame Pro model (though the previous version of that popular Art TV is on sale as well, see above). The Ember Artline has a matte display, includes the bezels and wall mounting brackets, and looks stunning. You can generate AI artwork using Alexa+ or match art images to your room decor.Photograph: Ryan WaniataPhotograph: Ryan WaniataPhotograph: Ryan WaniataPhotograph: Ryan WaniataHisenseU7SG QLED$1,500 $850 (43% off) Amazon$1,500 Best BuyOne of the best deals around this Prime Day is the Hisense U7 Mini-LED (2025). Powered by Google TV, the U7 has all the latest perks, like HDR10+, Dolby Vision IQ, Dolby Atmos, and IMAX Enhanced. For gamers, if you connect a high-end computer, the refresh rate can run as high as 165 Hz (or a 330 Hz variable refresh rate).Photograph: Ryan WaniataPhotograph: Ryan WaniataPhotograph: Ryan WaniataPhotograph: Ryan WaniataTCLQM6K QLED$600 $528 (12% off) Amazon$449 Amazon (55-inch)$650 $530 (18% off) Walmart$1,000 $650 (35% off) Best BuyMost PopularGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeGearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell DealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarThe already affordable TCL QM6K is a top seller, but at 20 percent off for Prime Day and priced at only $528, it’s quite a steal. The 65-inch model uses QLED display tech for bright colors and good contrast, even from a side viewing angle. Gamers will appreciate the 144 Hz refresh rate for low-latency performance, all powered by the intuitive Google TV operating system.LG ElectronicsLGC5$1,397 $1,199 (14% off) Amazon$2,700 $1,200 (56% off) Best BuyHome cinephiles willing to drop some cash for one of the best TVs this year will struggle to find a better option than the LG C5. It boasts perfect contrast, fantastic highlights, and vibrant color, and gets bright enough for well-lit rooms. It supports a 144-Hz refresh rate and Nvidia G-Sync for gamers, and there’s Dolby Vision for movie fans. We also love the LG Magic Remote, which makes it easy to navigate LG’s relatively good tvOS interface. This TV has been steadily dropping from its original $2,700 MSRP, but this is the lowest price yet.Photograph: Ryan WaniataPhotograph: Ryan WaniataPhotograph: Ryan WaniataSamsungS90F QD-OLED TV$1,698 $1,198 (29% off) AmazonShop atWalmart$2,500 $1,200 (52% off) SamsungMost PopularGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeGearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell DealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarThe best TV for gaming has hit a new low price. It is incredible accurate, offers near-infinite contrast, and boasts vibrant yet refined colors. It doesn't have Samsung’s matte-screen tech, but the darker backdrop keeps black levels rich, while still providing impressive glare reduction in moderately bright rooms. Samsung's gaming hub is the best around whether you're playing on a console or cloud gaming, and features an intuitive gaming bar, snappy input response, and a full suite of HDMI 2.1 ports.Deals on Streaming DevicesCourtesy of RokuRokuUltra (2024)$100 $85 (15% off) Amazon$100 Best BuyThe Roku Ultra streamer has premium features like Dolby Vision and HDR10+ for excellent picture quality, a backlit remote, and good performance. In fact, the Ultra is about 30 percent faster than other Roku streaming products. You can even connect Bluetooth headphones or speakers, and the device supports Dolby Atmos surround sound.Photograph: AmazonAmazonFire TV Stick 4K Max$60 $35 (42% off) AmazonMost PopularGearThe 99 Absolute Best Prime Day Deals on Our Favorite ProductsBy Louryn StrampeGearThe $25K Slate Is the Most Affordable EV Truck in the USBy Jeremy WhiteSmart HomeGoogle’s New Smart Speaker Takes the LeadBy Nena Farrell DealsYeti Tundra Coolers Are on Rare Sale TodayBy Martin CizmarTo bump up the storage available to you, the Fire TV Stick 4K Max version is the one you want. You can add more apps and spend less time managing the cache and memory with 16 GB of local storage. This streaming device can also automatically display artwork, similar to the Ember Artline but without the sublime matte display tech we loved in our review.Courtesy: AmazonAmazonFire TV Stick 4K Plus$25 AmazonAn early Prime Day deal, this 4K streaming stick is half off the normal price. It’s a smart purchase because the Fire TV Stick 4K Plus still supports 4K Ultra streaming, Dolby Vision and Atmos, and HDR10+ picture quality. The only missing feature is the ambient art mode that shows images automatically and comes with 8GB of storage instead of 16GB.Photograph: Parker HallGoogleTV Streamer (4K)$100 $75 (25% off) Amazon$99 $75 (24% off) Walmart$100 $75 (25% off) Best BuyOne of the best streamers around, the Google TV Streamer 4K is fast, is slim enough to fit anywhere on your TV stand, and has plenty of storage (at 32GB). Google TV offers an intuitive interface and easy voicebot search using Gemini. It’s available in gray or white.
